General support-function/minimal-statistic logic is routine partial-ID algebra; no checked SC paper states this endpoint map

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The scalar statistic q is ambiguous: Assumption anisotropic gives one equal-q pair, but the conjecture rules out all q in {||g||_2, ||g||_infty, RMSPE}; fix q or require anisotropy for each allowed q.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Quantify Conjecture 1 separately for each scalar statistic q, or state a fixed-q theorem plus corollaries for RMSPE, l2, and linfty.
Add an explicit nondegenerate 2D witness with X, b, rho, g1, g2 and computed support endpoints to make the genericity claim auditable.
Strengthen Conjecture 2 beyond the tautological endpoint-sufficiency implication by characterizing when common reporting statistics generate H on open classes.

recommended_next_step: Patch the scalar-q quantifier in Conjecture 1 and add the explicit anisotropic witness before Stage 0.
